tokens: (Matter-of-factly) Okay, Tony, starting on the deep drill.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That one went in like gangbusters!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we copy that, Charlie, and hold back on that drill a little bit.
It'll probably try to auger in on you a bit.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, that penetration rate was a little fast.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, if there is some way we could get that (heat flow) connector off of there, we might be able to take the whole...the electronics (back into the LM)...Naw, we can't do that either; the whole thing's hooked up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the LSM is on the surface.
I'm going to deploy the Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: These wires have...They have memory in them, and they stay crinkled up in odd manners here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: All right, John; we can sure see that.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Tony; I had a tough time getting the bit (means the drill) off the first stem.
Got a little dusty in there, but I got it cleaned out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I bet you that looks like a comedy of errors on the tube.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Nah; it's coming along fine there, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The problem is that the bit won't stay stuck in the ground; and when I try to get this stuff on, it spins the whole deal, instead of the...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That drill (means the wrench) is so good it's hard to get off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I mean, not the drill but the bit...(Correcting himself) the wrench.
Okay, second one going in, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm holding back on it this time.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the second one went in with no problem, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the Central Station is erected.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) And you're Go for the shorting switch when you get there (in the checklist as per
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That beauty just doesn't want to come off.
(Long Pause) Okay, last one (meaning the fourth drill stem) going on, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Somewhat out of breath) Then we'll see if we can get that beauty out of the ground.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, all the sections are like that first one (meaning that the stems turn in the hole when he tries to attach the next stem).
(Pause) (Thinking about extracting the core) (It feels like I could) pull it right out of the ground; but I don't think that's true.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I tell you, this ain't the cleanest place I've ever been in my life.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And I feel a little clutch slippage, but not much.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Just take it slow and easy.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Responding) That's what I'm doing; just letting it do the work.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Spin it free for 15 seconds without letting it go down if you can (as per
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I was just going to see if I was down far enough.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) I think I better use the jack.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Getting the wrench) When I was spinning it free, Tony, I felt like it was going to come right on out; but it's sort of hung up now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I got it out about one stem width...(Correcting himself) length.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: For some reason, that thing is hard to unscrew off.
(Pause) Hey, Tony, I'll save the drill just in case we can...y'all can come up with an answer on that heat flow.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, when you get that core out, we would like you to measure the hole with the rammer jammer.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Okay, right in here, Tony; we really sink in on that rim of that little crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And if you get tired there, Charlie, (while removing the core) just take a break.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Don't know how good I look.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think the antenna is aligned and pointing at you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And it's level; believe it or not.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Laughs) If the boss says that, I agree with him; yes, sir.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're going to push the shorting switch.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, the amps go to zero on the gauge.
Is that what they're supposed to do?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The top of the core - the deep core - has got cap number A.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, we've got a good ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I knew it was a good one.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, you can't believe it, Tony, but that beauty is coming out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And we've proved the lever principle again.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Let the suit do the work for you...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Looks like it's a good thing we had that jack.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I think, maybe, John and I'd have been able to pull it out; but it would have been a battle.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Still can't pull that beauty out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, is that (LSM) going to be all right next to that rock?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I don't have any idea, Charlie.
Sure hope so, 'cause there ain't much other place to put it.
If it ain't next to the rock, it's going to be in that hole right next to it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, I see what you mean.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This isn't the world's greatest place to deploy ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, how far are you from that rock?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You want to move it farther than that?
How far do you want to be?
I'll pick it up and move it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, I guess they would like you to try to move it away from that rock and, on the distance, just as far as you can without getting it in trouble there - jerking it around.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, can I pick it up by its arms and...If I promise to be careful with it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: They have re-evaluated the whole thing and decided, since you left...I guess they didn't want it (moved)...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...very badly, and they said just leave it where it is.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I can probably get down on my knees and get it (that is, take hold of it) underneath its little box...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: On that hole, there, instead of putting the rammer-jammer down it, I guess we would like to put the second heat flow probe down the hole and then measure it with the rammer-jammer, how far it went, and just leave the heat flow probe in the hole.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's right, the heat flow probe.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Or the...(Answering) No, I don't think it will reach, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, then, just go ahead and measure the hole (with the rammer-jammer).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Y'all going to give up on the heat flow?

tokens: Well, we'll probably have to; (we) haven't got a firm decision, yet.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'd like to save that stuff until you make a firm decision.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'd rather drill one (with the heat flow core stems).
I don't think it'll reach (core hole).

tokens: (Correcting himself) I mean the Sun is right in the middle of the alignment marks.

tokens: (With a smile in his voice) Outstanding!

tokens: I'd say the hole stayed open.

tokens: All the way down, I just dropped the rammer into it and it just fell in.
